records.ety: [F. concentrer, fr. L. con- + centrum center. See Center, and cf. Concentrate]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Concentered or Concentred (<?/); p. pr & vb. n. Concentering (?) or Concentring (<?/).]

defs: To come to one point; to meet in, or converge toward, a common center; to have a common center.
quotes: God, in whom all perfections concenter.

defs: To draw or direct to a common center; to bring together at a focus or point, as two or more lines; to concentrate.
quotes: In thee concentering all their precious beams.
All is concentered in a life intense.
